goal: Optimize the Orbits local-remote relationship database schema against the advisory AI relationship design without blindly copying it or preserving inefficient current gaps.

success_criteria.description: The implementation documents a concrete field-by-field schema decision for event intent, AI analyses, relationship profiles, interaction memory, recommendations, golden matches, negative cases, and dirty cases: keep existing product fields, promote frequently queried values to top-level fields, keep volatile AI output in resultJson-style semantic records, and reject redundant or low-value fields with stated reasons.

The local-remote database schema version is advanced and the seeded runtime state exposes typed collections or fields for event participant intent, AI analyses, match recommendations, interaction memories, and recommendation-test records while preserving the existing accounts, profiles, events, attendees, contacts, connections, evidence, tasks, conversations, messages, dashboards, agent actions, permissions, and notifications collections.

Event-specific looking-for and can-offer intent, preferred language, relationship strength, trust level, business relevance score, shared topics, and suggested actions are queryable without parsing AI result JSON; tests prove these fields exist on the right records and remain source/evidence backed.

AI analysis, match recommendation, interaction memory, golden match, negative case, and dirty data records include stable ids, target references, source/evidence references, confidence or scoring where relevant, and tests prove cross-record references resolve to seeded events, contacts, attendees, connections, evidence, or profiles.

The mock-to-live replacement document explains future remote tables, indexes, JSON fields, privacy/provenance constraints, migration/version behavior, and replacement tests; the focused relationship schema test command and npm run lint pass after the schema update.

out_of_scope: Replacing the local-remote store with Supabase, Postgres, Prisma, IndexedDB, or a live provider
Adding authentication, network sync, vector search, embeddings, or live AI calls to the Orbits app
Changing product routes, app shell, feature page UI, API behavior, or external side-effect boundaries
Generating the large v1 demo data files under repos/mockdata; that belongs to Sprint 82
